reason: The remediation only adds the sticky bit to world-writable directories, which is a standard security hardening measure. It does not modify authentication, networking, or boot-critical components, and the risk of locking out access is minimal. While some applications may rely on the absence of the sticky bit, this can be assessed per directory before applying the change.
estimated_impact: filesystems affected; Potential for an application to misbehave if it expects a non-sticky directory; Broad filesystem scan may affect many directories, requiring validation
